dimension_notes.naming_quality: Consistent kebab-case file naming throughout. Variable and function names are descriptive. Pure helper functions (applyStreamComplete, applyStreamError, etc.) follow clear verb+noun convention. Factory function names could use more domain-intent names but are functional.
dimension_notes.logic_clarity: Streaming handlers simplified via extracted pure helpers. Route handler logic uses clear early returns. mergeServerData helper eliminates repetitive state update patterns. Some route handlers still have long sequential validation chains.
dimension_notes.type_safety: Gateway-client.ts has typed payload interfaces (MessageNewPayload, StreamStartPayload, etc.). Pure helper functions have explicit input/output types. Some route handlers still use inline type assertions after JSON parsing.
dimension_notes.contract_coherence: PROTOCOL.md is comprehensive and up to date. Typed payload interfaces in gateway-client.ts make contracts explicit. Some implicit contracts between web and gateway could still be more explicit with runtime validation.
dimension_notes.error_consistency: DELETE endpoints consistently return {success: true}. Error responses follow {error: string} pattern. console.error logging is consistent with [module] prefix pattern. Security-sensitive error messages no longer leak secret names.
dimension_notes.abstraction_fitness: Handler registration pattern well-extracted. mergeServerData helper eliminates 4 repetitive state update patterns. 6 pure streaming helpers extracted from use-channel.ts. HandlerDeps interface could be split into narrower interfaces.
dimension_notes.ai_generated_debt: Codebase significantly cleaned up over multiple sessions. Repetitive patterns reduced via mergeServerData and streaming helpers. Some repetitive fetch-parse-error patterns remain in ChatProvider refresh functions.
dimension_notes.high_level_elegance: Clean separation: Gateway=transport, Go=orchestration, Web=API+UI. Package boundaries are well-defined. No circular dependencies between packages.
dimension_notes.mid_level_elegance: ChatProvider improved with mergeServerData. Streaming lifecycle well-structured with extracted pure helpers. Some components still have high complexity but manage genuinely complex state.
dimension_notes.low_level_elegance: Pure helper functions extracted from inline expressions improve readability. Variable renames (webhookToken, generatedKey) improve clarity. Some inline type casts remain in route handlers.
dimension_notes.cross_module_architecture: Clear package boundaries. Gateway client abstracts HTTP transport cleanly. CLI package properly isolated. Shared types package keeps common interfaces DRY.
dimension_notes.initialization_coupling: Environment variable reads are at call-time in socket.ts. Module-level state is minimal. PrismaClient uses globalThis singleton pattern correctly for Next.js hot reload.
dimension_notes.convention_outlier: File naming consistently kebab-case. Test files use consistent patterns with vi.mock and test fixtures. Some variation in mock setup approaches across test files.
dimension_notes.dependency_health: Dependencies well-managed. No circular imports detected. Package boundaries clean. Phoenix JS dependency should be version-pinned.
dimension_notes.test_strategy: Significant improvement: 100+ tests across 12+ test suites. New tests cover admin-auth, auth config, check-member-permission, db singleton, env validation, image-dimensions, rate-limit, socket, gateway-client, use-channel-helpers. Coverage gaps remain in UI components and providers.
dimension_notes.api_surface_coherence: DELETE endpoints standardized. Route handler signatures consistent. Internal API uses typed interfaces. Some list endpoints return arrays directly while others wrap in named properties.
dimension_notes.authorization_consistency: Auth patterns consistent: session check, permission check, then action. Admin token authentication uses timing-safe comparison. Owner-only checks could be more unified across endpoints.
dimension_notes.incomplete_migration: PascalCase to kebab-case migration complete. All .js files migrated to .ts. Sequence type handling still varies between string and bigint in some places.
dimension_notes.package_organization: Clean package structure: web, shared, cli, gateway, streaming. Auth files follow consistent naming. Components well-organized in feature directories. E2E tests within web package is acceptable for monorepo.
dimension_notes.design_coherence: Consistent patterns for route handlers, streaming lifecycle, and broadcast helpers. State management uses both direct setState and mergeServerData patterns, which could be consolidated.

issues.suggestion: Consider using zod or similar for runtime body validation in route handlers.
issues.summary: Route handlers use inline 'as' type assertions for request body parsing
evidence: Route handlers use 'as Record<string, unknown>' after JSON parsing rather than runtime validation with schemas.

issues.suggestion: Add component tests for critical UI components using testing-library.
issues.summary: UI components and providers lack test coverage
evidence: While lib modules now have good test coverage, UI components and providers still have no unit tests. The mergeServerData helper in chat-provider.tsx is tested indirectly but component behavior is not.

issues.suggestion: Extract a field validation helper or use a schema validation library for route handlers.
issues.summary: Channel PATCH handler has a long sequential validation chain
evidence: The PATCH handler validates 7+ fields sequentially with similar patterns.

issues.suggestion: Establish a standard mock pattern and document it in a testing guide.
issues.summary: Test files use slightly different mocking approaches
evidence: Some tests use vi.stubGlobal, others use vi.mock with factory functions, and some use inline mocks.

issues.suggestion: Standardize list response shape to always wrap in a named property.
issues.summary: List endpoints have inconsistent response shapes
evidence: Some list endpoints wrap results in {members: [...]} while others return bare arrays.

issues.suggestion: Consider consolidating state management to use mergeServerData pattern consistently.
issues.summary: State updates use both direct setState and mergeServerData patterns
evidence: ChatProvider uses mergeServerData for some updates but direct setState for channels/members/agents in some paths.

issues.suggestion: Pin phoenix dependency to exact version to prevent unexpected WebSocket protocol changes.
issues.summary: Phoenix JS client dependency should be version-pinned
evidence: The phoenix package dependency uses a semver range rather than an exact version lock.

issues.suggestion: Standardize error logging format to always use [module] prefix.
issues.summary: Error logging uses slightly different formats across handlers
evidence: Some error logs use template literals, others string concatenation, and formatting of context varies.

issues.suggestion: Consider splitting HandlerDeps into narrower interfaces per handler group.
issues.summary: HandlerDeps interface bundles 15+ unrelated concerns
evidence: HandlerDeps bundles refs, setters, and utility functions into one interface making it unclear which handler uses which deps.

issues.suggestion: Extract a generic fetchAndSet helper to DRY up the refresh functions.
issues.summary: ChatProvider repeats identical fetch-parse-error patterns in refresh functions
evidence: refreshChannels, refreshMembers, and refreshAgents follow nearly identical patterns of fetch, check res.ok, parse JSON, set state, catch error.

issues.suggestion: Consider a unified isOwner check or ensuring owner always has all permissions.
issues.summary: Owner-only checks vary in implementation across endpoints
evidence: Server DELETE checks server.ownerId directly while other endpoints use checkMemberPermission.

issues.suggestion: Add runtime validation for gateway broadcast payloads or document shapes in PROTOCOL.md.
issues.summary: Gateway broadcast contract is only implicitly defined through TypeScript interfaces
evidence: Broadcast payload shapes are defined in TypeScript but not validated at runtime against the Elixir gateway.

issues.suggestion: Extract gateway URL construction into a config module.
issues.summary: Web package constructs gateway internal API URLs directly
evidence: gateway-client.ts constructs URLs like /api/internal/broadcast directly, coupling to gateway URL structure.

issues.suggestion: Consider routing webhook agent SSE through Go streaming proxy for consistency.
issues.summary: Some streaming orchestration exists in both web API and streaming proxy
evidence: The webhook dispatch route handles SSE stream reading and token relaying, overlapping with Go streaming proxy responsibility.

issues.suggestion: Standardize sequence type handling to always use string representation.
issues.summary: Sequence values are sometimes numbers, sometimes strings across the codebase
evidence: fetchChannelSequence returns string but some callers and DB models expect bigint.

issues.suggestion: Consider using a class or context-based approach for socket lifecycle management.
issues.summary: socket.ts uses module-level singleton state
evidence: Socket and currentToken are module-level singletons coupling connection lifecycle to module initialization.

issues.suggestion: Replace inline type assertions with proper type narrowing or zod parsing.
issues.summary: Some inline type casts remain in route handlers
evidence: Route handlers use (avatarUrl as string) and similar inline casts rather than proper type narrowing.

issues.suggestion: Consider splitting into separate providers for server data, permissions, and routing.
issues.summary: ChatProvider manages many concerns in a single component
evidence: ChatProvider handles server list, channel list, members, agents, permissions, and routing in one component.

issues.suggestion: Consider domain-intent names if touching these functions in future.
issues.summary: Route handler factory functions use method-centric naming
evidence: Functions like createInternalMessagesPostHandler describe HTTP method rather than domain action.

issues.suggestion: Consider moving e2e tests to a top-level tests/ directory if the project grows.
issues.summary: E2E test helpers are inside the web package
evidence: The e2e/ directory lives inside packages/web rather than at the monorepo root.
